title: Eklavya temple
tagline: Eklavya temple — spiritual town of Haryana

overview.short: The Ekalavya temple (Hindi: एकलव्य मंदिर) is the only Hindu temple in the world dedicated to Ekalavya of the Mahabharata.
overview.description: The Ekalavya temple (Hindi: एकलव्य मंदिर) is the only Hindu temple in the world dedicated to Ekalavya of the Mahabharata. It is located in the Khandsa village of Gurugram, Haryana, India. It is built atop the spot where Ekalavya cut his thumb and offered it to Guru Drona as Guru Dakshina. His thumb was buried in this location and a temple was built on top of it to honor this great hero.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Khandsa
topPlaces.description: Khandsa is a village in Gurgaon mandal in Gurgaon District, Haryana State, India. It has a population of about 9959 persons living in some 1912 households.

topPlaces.name: Begampur Khatola
topPlaces.description: Begumpur Khatola is a village in Gurugram Mandal, Gurugram District, in the Indian state of Haryana. It lies on National Highway 8 on Milestone 39 next to Hero Honda Chowk.

topPlaces.name: Sihi
topPlaces.description: Sihi is a village panchayat located in the Gurgaon district of the Indian state of Haryana. The village is dominated by dalits.

topPlaces.name: Kherki Daula
topPlaces.description: Kherki Daula is a village in Gurgaon on National Highway 48 (India). Its Pincode is 122004.

topPlaces.name: Bhim Sena
topPlaces.description: The Bhim Sena, alternatively Bheem Sena, or Akhil Bhartiya Bhim Sena, abbreviated as ABBS, lit."All India Ambedkar Army", is an Ambedkarite social organization working for the rights of Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, Other Backward Classes and religious minorities in India under the leadership of the organisation's national president Satpal Tanwar. The organisation is pro reservation and is named after B. R. Ambedkar.

topPlaces.name: Chakkarpur
topPlaces.description: Chakkarpur is a large village located in Sector 28 of Gurugram, Haryana, India, along the Mehrauli–Gurugram Road. Known for its peaceful environment, the village is predominantly inhabited by members of the Yadav(Gotra Nuniwal) community.

topPlaces.name: Fazilpur Jharsa
topPlaces.description: Fazilpur Jharsa is a village in the Gurgaon mandal of Gurgaon district in the state of Haryana in India. It is located on the Sohna Road.

topPlaces.name: Basai
topPlaces.description: Basai is a village in Gurgaon Mandal, Gurgaon District, Haryana, India. Basai is 4 km distance from its district main city Gurgaon and 282 km distance from its state main city Chandigarh.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Eklavya temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Eklavya temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Eklavya temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Indira Gandhi International Airport, Delhi (18 km) and the nearest railway station is New Delhi Railway Station (33 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Eklavya temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Eklavya temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Eklavya temple?
faq.a: Stays in Eklavya temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Eklavya temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Khandsa, Begampur Khatola, Sihi, Kherki Daula, Bhim Sena.
